title: refactor(enter): remove dead exports across util, events, middleware, and cache
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- util.ts: drop 10 unused exports (generateRandomId, batches, omit, joinOptionalStrings, applyStyle/AnsiStyle/ansiStyles, lines, safeRound, withRetry/RetryOptions) plus the now-orphaned nanoid id helper
- events.ts: remove dead sendToTinybird + flattenBalances and the unused TinybirdEvent import + capitalize import; update events.test.ts (drop 5 now-dead tests)
- middleware/balance.ts: remove unused requirePositiveBalance/requirePaidBalance + fetchBalanceWithErrorHandling helper and trim shared balance-check imports; keep getBalance/getAvailableBalance/UserBalance
- middleware/auth.ts: remove unused requireModelAccess/requireKeyBudget and the ModelVariables augmentation; keep requireAuthorization/requireUser
- delete unused middleware/alias.ts (zero importers) and dead hashArgs fallback in cache.ts

Risks: none — pure dead-code removal, no callers, no behavior change. getBalance path never used the deleted 503 wrapper, so customer.ts behavior is unchanged.

_~505 net lines removed · biome ✓ · cd enter.pollinations.ai && npx vitest run test/events.test.ts test/balance-check.test.ts  ✓_

gist.headline: Spring cleaning the hive
gist.blurb: Swept away over 500 lines of unused code from our API gateway. A lighter codebase means faster development and fewer bugs hiding in the shadows!
gist.summary: Removed over 500 lines of dead code and unused exports across the enter.pollinations.ai utility, events, middleware, and cache modules.
gist.impact: Developers benefit from a cleaner, more maintainable codebase with no changes to existing API behavior.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot sweeping up dead leaves or throwing away old, dusty code blocks into a recycling bin. Nomnom creature eating the old code.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny janitor hat, sweeping up dusty, gray pixelated code blocks. The round tan nomnom creature happily eats the pile of discarded code. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
